title: Sorting with ORDER BY

theory.introduction: The ORDER BY clause is used to sort the result set in ascending or descending order based on one or more columns.

sections.title: Basic ORDER BY Syntax
sections.content:
By default, ORDER BY sorts in ascending order (ASC):

SELECT name, population
FROM countries
ORDER BY population;

This is the same as:

SELECT name, population
FROM countries
ORDER BY population ASC;

sections.title: Descending Order
sections.content:
Use DESC to sort in descending order:

SELECT name, population
FROM countries
ORDER BY population DESC;

sections.title: Sorting by Multiple Columns
sections.content:
You can sort by multiple columns. The second column is used to break ties:

SELECT name, continent, population
FROM countries
ORDER BY continent ASC, population DESC;

This sorts by continent first, then by population (descending) within each continent.

sections.title: LIMIT and OFFSET
sections.content:
Use LIMIT to restrict the number of rows returned:

-- Get top 5 most populous countries
SELECT name, population
FROM countries
ORDER BY population DESC
LIMIT 5;

Use OFFSET to skip a number of rows (useful for pagination):

-- Get rows 6-10
SELECT name, population
FROM countries
ORDER BY population DESC
LIMIT 5 OFFSET 5;

examples.title: Sort by population descending
examples.description: Get countries ordered by population (highest first)
examples.code: SELECT name, population FROM countries ORDER BY population DESC;

examples.title: Sort by multiple columns
examples.description: Sort by continent, then by name within each continent
examples.code: SELECT name, continent FROM countries ORDER BY continent, name;

examples.title: Get top 3 results
examples.code: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 3;

exercise.instruction: Write a query to find the top 5 highest-paid employees. Select first_name, last_name, and salary from the employees and salaries tables. For now, just use the salaries table and order by salary descending, limiting to 5 results.
exercise.hint: SELECT the columns from salaries table, ORDER BY salary DESC, and use LIMIT 5 to get only the top 5.
exercise.solution: SELECT emp_no, salary FROM salaries ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 5;

learningObjectives: Sort in ascending (ASC) and descending (DESC) order

quiz.question: What is the default sort order of ORDER BY?
quiz.explanation: ORDER BY sorts in ascending order (ASC) by default. You must explicitly specify DESC for descending order.
